title: hemerocallis fulva var. longituba
intro: Hemerocallis fulva var. longituba, commonly known as the Orange Daylily, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Hemerocallidaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Hemerocallis fulva var. longituba include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and treat various respiratory issues, making it a valuable herb in herbal medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
